eggplant-burger-4-recipes-pequerecetas.name: Eggplant Burger (4 Recipes)

ingredients.item: Aceite de oliva para untar la bandeja

instructions: It purifies the oven at 180 and 3. Fits a plate with olive oil. Wash the eggplants, cut them in half, cut them and bake them for 25 minutes till they are well cooked. Let them cool.
With a spoon, remove his pulp from his eggplants to a big bowl. Unskin. Add onion, garlic, oatmeal, seeds and spices. Saxony with salt and pepper. Fold well.
The mix forms 6 medium-sized burgers and puts them on the plate. Bake the burgers for 15- 20 minutes, turn them half the time. Serves hot.

eggplant-caponata-caponata-siciliana-arg.name: Eggplant Caponata
instructions: For eggplant caponata, heat the olive oil first in a large pan.
Add the eggs cut into small cubes and leave them on high heat to Wilt and soften for a few minutes.
Add the onion chopped, the saddle chopped and all the other injectors.
Cover and simmer for about 15 minutes, stringing regularly.
Finally, salt and pepper. The materials must not melt. They need low fire to keep their shape.
Serve the caponata warm as pasta sauce or Frozen as salad, on toasted bread with feta.

emmas-fudgy-chocolate-pie.name: Emma's Fudgy Chocolate Pie
instructions: Cream sugar and yolks together in a large saucepan. Add milk, cocoa powder, cornstarch, and salt; whisk until no lumps remain. Add half-and-half and heat over medium heat, whisking constantly until filling is thickened, about 20 minutes.

Turn off heat when filling starts to bubble; continue to stir for 1 minute. Remove thickened filling from the warm burner. Add butter and vanilla extract, stirring well until melted and well incorporated. Let filling sit to cool slightly, about 10 minutes.
Stir filling well and pour into graham cracker crust, smoothing the top with a spatula. Chill, uncovered, in the refrigerator for 4 hours.

extra-crispy-spicy-fried-cauliflower.name: Extra Crispy Spicy Fried Cauliflower
instructions: Cut the cauliflower in large florets. Transfer to a large bowl and set aside.
In a small bowl, combine the celery salt, oregano, cumin, onion powder, garlic powder, paprika, cayenne, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
In a medium bowl, add half of the spice mix to the flour.
Add the cornstarch and mix.
In a separate medium bowl, combine the soy milk, apple cider vinegar, and hot sauce. Whisk.
Pour the remaining spice mix over the cauliflower florets and mix.
Pour the milk mixture over the cauliflower and mix well until they are coated.
Cover with plastic wrap and chill for 1 hour minimum.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan until it reaches 177°C (180°C).

Toss each cauliflower floret in the flour mixture until well-coated, shaking off any excess. Dip them back in the remaining soy milk mixture in the bowl where they marinated, then again in the flour mixture.
Fry the cauliflower for 4-5 minutes, until golden brown and crispy.
Drain the pieces on a wire or paper towels. Let cool 5-10 minutes before serving.
Enjoy!
